elf.description: An immortal race with pointed ears, renowned for craftsmanship, poetry, and skill with a bow. They have lived in Middle-earth since the First Age.

wizard.description: One of the five Istari sent to Middle-earth in mortal form. Appears as an old man carrying a staff, with powers over fire, light, and the minds of others.

ring wraith.description: Formerly great kings of Men, now wraith-like servants of Sauron bound to the Nine Rings. They ride black horses and inspire dread in all living things.

orc/uruk-hai.description: Brutal warrior breed created by Saruman by crossing orcs with men. Larger, stronger, and able to travel in daylight — the shock troops of Isengard.

hobbit.description: Small, stout folk of the Shire, averaging three and a half feet tall. Known for their love of food, pipe-weed, and a quiet life — rarely seen beyond their own borders.

dwarf.description: Short, stocky, long-lived miners and smiths who dwell in mountain halls. Fiercely proud of their clans, their craft, and their beards.

tree.description: Ancient tree-herders of Fangorn Forest, the oldest living things in Middle-earth. Slow to speak, slow to decide, and formidable when roused to action.

human.description: The mortal race spread across Middle-earth in many kingdoms. Shorter-lived than elves or dwarves, but ambitious, adaptable, and numerous.

gollum.description: Once a Stoor hobbit named Sméagol, warped by five centuries of carrying the One Ring. Lives alone in caves, surviving on raw fish and speaking to himself in two voices.

dragon.description: Enormous fire-breathing reptiles of great cunning and greed. Dragons hoard gold beneath mountains and can speak — though rarely to anyone's benefit.

eagle.description: Giant eagles of the Misty Mountains, among the largest flying creatures in Middle-earth. They intervene rarely, but decisively, at critical moments.

warg.description: Large, wolf-like predators used as mounts by orcs. They hunt in packs and are found throughout the Misty Mountains and beyond.

giant spider.description: Descendants of Ungoliant, dwelling in dark places such as Mirkwood and Cirith Ungol. They spin webs to trap prey and can grow to enormous size.

valar.description: The fourteen angelic powers who shaped the world under Ilúvatar. Each governs a domain — sea, sky, earth, craft — and dwells in the Undying Lands of Valinor.

maiar.description: Lesser angelic spirits, including Gandalf, Saruman, and the Balrogs. They can take physical form and walk among mortals, though their true nature is far older than the world.
